DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api artifact-reference best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Status Definition

The lifecycle status of a design system artifact and optional platform-specific readiness tracking. Used across all DSDS document types to indicate maturity and readiness. The statusObject consolidates status, platformStatus, and deprecationNotice into a single reusable object with a built-in conditional requiring deprecationNotice when status is 'deprecated'.

Source: common/status.schema.json

3 definitions in this file:

  • statusValue
  • platformStatusEntry
  • statusObject

statusValue

A lifecycle status value. Standard values: 'draft' (under development, not ready for use), 'experimental' (available but API may change without notice), 'stable' (production-ready, changes follow semver), 'deprecated' (scheduled for removal). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case (e.g., 'sunset', 'archived', 'beta').

string

platformStatusEntry

The readiness status of an artifact on a specific platform. Tracks lifecycle stage, version availability, and optional deprecation notices independently per platform.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
statusstatusValuerequiredThe lifecycle status of the artifact on this platform. Uses the same vocabulary as the top-level status: 'draft', 'experimental', 'stable', 'deprecated'. Custom values are permitted.
sincestringoptionalThe version in which this artifact became available on this platform.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'. Explains what to use instead on this platform.
descriptionstringoptionalOptional notes about the artifact's status on this platform (e.g., 'Available as a Web Component wrapper; native implementation planned for v4').

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: statusValue

Example

[
  {
    "status": "stable",
    "since": "1.0.0"
  },
  {
    "status": "experimental",
    "since": "3.2.0",
    "description": "Available as a Web Component wrapper. Native shadow DOM implementation planned for v4."
  },
  {
    "status": "draft",
    "description": "Compose implementation in progress. Expected in v4.0."
  },
  {
    "status": "deprecated",
    "since": "1.5.0",
    "deprecationNotice": "The Vue wrapper is deprecated. Use the Web Component directly in Vue applications."
  }
]

statusObject

The consolidated lifecycle status of an artifact. Contains the overall status value, optional per-platform readiness tracking, and a conditional deprecation notice. Every artifact that carries status uses this single object rather than spreading status, platformStatus, and deprecationNotice as separate sibling properties. When the overall status is 'deprecated', the deprecationNotice property becomes required.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
statusstatusValuerequiredThe overall lifecycle status of the artifact. Represents an editorial judgment about the artifact's maturity — it is not required to be the most or least advanced of the platform statuses. Tools SHOULD display this as the primary status indicator.
platformStatusmap<string, platformStatusEntry>optionalPlatform-specific readiness or availability status. Keys are platform identifiers (freeform strings — common values include 'react', 'web-component', 'ios', 'android', 'flutter', 'figma', 'sketch', and 'compose'). Values describe the artifact's status on that platform independently. An artifact MAY be 'stable' overall while individual platforms are still 'experimental' or 'draft'.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'. MUST explain what to use instead and provide a migration path. When present, tools SHOULD display this prominently alongside the status indicator.

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: statusValue, platformStatusEntry

Example

[
  {
    "status": "stable"
  },
  {
    "status": "stable",
    "platformStatus": {
      "react": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "1.0.0"
      },
      "web-component": {
        "status": "experimental",
        "since": "3.2.0",
        "description": "Available as a Web Component wrapper. Native shadow DOM implementation planned for v4."
      },
      "ios": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "2.1.0"
      },
      "android": {
        "status": "draft",
        "description": "Compose implementation in progress. Expected in v4.0."
      },
      "figma": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "1.0.0"
      },
      "vue": {
        "status": "deprecated",
        "since": "1.5.0",
        "deprecationNotice": "The Vue wrapper is deprecated. Use the Web Component directly in Vue applications."
      }
    }
  },
  {
    "status": "deprecated",
    "deprecationNotice": "This component is deprecated. Use NewComponent instead. See the migration guide at https://design.acme.com/migrations/old-to-new."
  },
  {
    "status": "experimental",
    "platformStatus": {
      "react": {
        "status": "experimental",
        "since": "4.0.0-beta.1"
      },
      "figma": {
        "status": "draft",
        "description": "Figma component is in design review. Not yet available in the library."
      },
      "a11y-keyboard": {
        "status": "stable",
        "description": "Passes all automated keyboard navigation tests."
      },
      "a11y-screen-reader": {
        "status": "stable",
        "description": "Manually tested with JAWS, VoiceOver, and NVDA."
      }
    }
  }
]
↑ Back to top
